steps.title: Interpret 4% annual increase
steps.content: When the population increases by 4% each year, the new population is 100% + 4% = 104% of the previous year. As a multiplier, that's 1.04.

steps.title: Build the exponential growth equation
steps.content:
Starting population in 2008: 5,000. After $x$ years of multiplying by 1.04 each year:
  • Year 1: $5,000 \times 1.04$
  • Year 2: $5,000 \times (1.04)^2$
  • Year $x$: $5,000 \times (1.04)^x$
The general form is $p = 5,000(1.04)^x$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
